title: Coconut cream pie
subtitle: Coconut pastry cream in a blind-baked case with toasted coconut and whipped cream
excerpt: A blind-baked shortcrust case filled with a thick coconut pastry cream made with coconut milk, topped with lightly whipped cream and toasted desiccated coconut. Rich, cold, and deeply coconutty.
sourceNotes: Coconut cream pie is a standard of American diner baking and community cookbooks, appearing widely from the mid-twentieth century. The combination of coconut milk custard, a baked case, whipped cream, and toasted coconut is documented across American household cooking tradition. The recipe here follows the classic American approach, using coconut milk in place of plain milk for the pastry cream.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Both the pastry case and the coconut cream can be made the day before. Assemble the pie up to 4 hours before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven for blind baking. Fan at 170°C.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pastry cream made using full-fat coconut milk in place of dairy milk. The coconut fat produces a cream that is firmer when cold than a dairy version, and the flavour is pronounced — use full-fat tinned coconut milk, not reduced-fat or coconut drinking milk.

Desiccated coconut toasted in a dry pan or under a grill until golden and fragrant. It burns quickly, so stir constantly and remove from heat as soon as it colours. Adds a nutty depth to the raw coconut flavour.

content.text: made entirely with coconut milk — no dairy. The result is firm, sliceable, and deeply flavoured. The
scattered over the top adds texture and contrast against the soft cream.
Make and blind bake the pastry case

Rub the butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolk and water and bring together. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 190°C conventional. Roll the pastry to 3 mm and line a 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin. Line with baking paper and fill with baking beans. Bake for 20 minutes, remove the beans and paper, and bake for a further 8 to 10 minutes until the base is dry and pale gold. Cool completely.

Whisk the egg yolks, sugar, and cornflour together. Bring the coconut milk and whole milk to a simmer. Pour a third over the egg mixture while whisking to temper, then return to the pan and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until very thick. Stir in the desiccated coconut. Press cling film directly onto the surface and refrigerate until cold.

Toast the 30 g desiccated coconut in a dry pan until golden. Set aside to cool. Beat the cold coconut pastry cream briefly to loosen it and spoon into the baked pastry case, smoothing the top. Spoon or pipe the lightly whipped cream over the filling. Scatter the toasted coconut over the top. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.
